title: Four rows: singles, pairs, triples, all-four — organic tableau

rule.instruction: A wall is divided into four horizontal parts. In the top row are four equal divisions, each with lines in a different direction. In the second row, six double combinations; in the third row, four triple combinations; in the bottom row, all four combinations superimposed.

restrictions: Faithful structure: four horizontal rows — top row = the 4 single directions (4 cells), second row = the 6 double combinations (6 cells), third row = the 4 triple combinations (4 cells), bottom row = all four superimposed. By construction this is a TOP-LIGHT -> BOTTOM-DARK tonal tableau. Executed through the governing organic taste: all HAND-DRAWN (jitter), FINE/DENSE, asymmetric per-cell seeds. Per round-9 lesson, the 16 push EXTREMES (very open vs very dense spacing, gentle vs wild hand) and shuffle the cell orderings, to fight within-round sameness — the last combination-grid before the organic 'not straight' instructions.
